description:
I need to verify the integrity of a backup file that was created yesterday. The backup file is located at /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql.gz and I need to make sure it hasn't been corrupted.

Please do the following:

1. First, calculate the MD5 checksum of the compressed backup file /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql.gz

2. Then decompress the file and calculate the MD5 checksum of the uncompressed contents (without actually creating a new file - just pipe the decompressed output to the checksum tool)

3. Create a verification log file at /home/user/backups/integrity_check.log with exactly the following format:

```
BACKUP INTEGRITY REPORT
=======================
File: /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql.gz
Compressed MD5: <the md5 hash of the compressed file>
Uncompressed MD5: <the md5 hash of the uncompressed content>
Status: VERIFIED
```

The MD5 hashes should be exactly 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters. Make sure the log file has exactly 6 lines with the format shown above, including the header line and the separator line of equals signs (23 equals signs total).
truth:
Setup required before task:
- Create directory: /home/user/backups/
- Create a test SQL file and compress it:
  - First create /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql with content:
    ```
    -- Database backup created 2024-01-15
    CREATE TABLE users (id INT, name VARCHAR(100));
    INSERT INTO users VALUES (1, 'Alice');
    INSERT INTO users VALUES (2, 'Bob');
    ```
  - Then compress it with gzip to create /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql.gz
  - Remove the original .sql file after compression

Expected values for verification:
- Compressed file path: /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql.gz
- Compressed MD5: d4a0e5d2c7a5f8b3e9c1d2a4b6c8e0f2 (this will be computed from actual file)
- The verification log must be at: /home/user/backups/integrity_check.log

Verification log format requirements:
- Line 1: "BACKUP INTEGRITY REPORT"
- Line 2: "=======================" (exactly 23 equals signs)
- Line 3: "File: /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql.gz"
- Line 4: "Compressed MD5: " followed by 32 hex characters
- Line 5: "Uncompressed MD5: " followed by 32 hex characters
- Line 6: "Status: VERIFIED"
- Total lines: exactly 6

Commands the agent might use:
- md5sum /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql.gz
- zcat /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql.gz | md5sum
- Or gunzip -c /home/user/backups/database_dump.sql.gz | md5sum

The actual MD5 values will depend on the exact content of the created file. The test should:
1. Verify the log file exists at /home/user/backups/integrity_check.log
2. Verify it has exactly 6 lines
3. Verify line 1 is "BACKUP INTEGRITY REPORT"
4. Verify line 2 is "======================="
5. Verify line 3 contains the correct file path
6. Verify line 4 has a valid 32-character hex MD5 hash
7. Verify line 5 has a valid 32-character hex MD5 hash
8. Verify line 6 is "Status: VERIFIED"
9. Verify the compressed MD5 in the log matches actual md5sum of the .gz file
10. Verify the uncompressed MD5 in the log matches zcat piped to md5sum
name: task_000000_a9708205
